characters.description: A man brought from Earth to the world called the Land, where he holds the rare status of a chaos seed. As this book opens he is lord of a young settlement, the Mist Village, in the River Peninsula, and an ally of the wood sprites. He is still building his people's defenses and mastering the world's system-driven magic and skills.
characters.name: Richter

characters.description: A young wood sprite, son of the Hearth Mother Hisako, and Richter's close companion. An archer and woodsman proud of his people's ways, he has traveled and fought at Richter's side since their earlier adventures.
characters.name: Sion

characters.description: The Hearth Mother who leads the hearth-tree wood sprites in the Forest of Nadria, and Sion's mother. A formidable mage of life and light magic, she has pledged an alliance of mutual aid with Richter and his village.
characters.name: Hisako

characters.description: A warrior companion of Richter, of mixed wood-sprite and human heritage, and one of the most skilled swordsmen among Hisako's people. Blunt, hard-driving, and quick-tempered, he takes on the task of training Richter with the blade.
characters.name: Yoshi

characters.description: A female wood sprite who joins Richter's circle near the hearth tree. Spirited and teasing, she is especially fond of needling Sion, whom she likes to address by a formal name he dislikes. A capable archer, she travels with the party to the Mist Village.
characters.name: Daniella

characters.description: An elder wood sprite and master of life magic, one of Hisako's most trusted advisors. She travels with Richter's party as an ambassador to the Mist Village and begins teaching Richter the fundamentals of life magic.
characters.name: Sumiko

characters.description: A remnant, a fragment of an ancient mage's soul bound to serve whoever leads the Mist Village's place of power. Appearing as a small floating gray orb with a flat, deadpan voice, he manages village logistics and identifies magic items for Richter, and reasons with surprising sharpness.
characters.name: Futen

characters.description: A companion of Richter and a former innkeeper from Law, the capital of Yves, who has been running the Mist Village day to day in Richter's absence. He is worn down by grief over a friend lost during their escape from Yves and by caring for his traumatized love, Isabel.
characters.name: Terrod

characters.description: An herbalist of the Mist Village and Terrod's love. She is introduced as deeply traumatized in the wake of the group's flight from Yves; she is spoken of but does not appear directly when first mentioned.
characters.name: Isabel

characters.description: Richter's chamberlain, who runs the Mist Village's administration from an office in its catacombs. Formal and meticulous, and quietly irritated when Richter calls him Randy. He once served the old King of Yves monitoring court correspondence, and possesses a code-breaking skill Richter puts to use on stolen documents.
characters.name: Randolphus

characters.description: Richter's soul familiar, a small dragon-like creature summoned through a rare one-time tome and bonded to him for life by the Psi Bond, an ability that lets the two share thoughts and images. Established as female, she is willful, food-driven, and affectionate by turns.
characters.name: Alma

characters.description: A count in the court of the new King of Yves, part of the king's inner circle and disliked even by old-regime standards. He does not appear directly at first; Richter has robbed his safe of coded papers and other valuables, and the count is tied to Isabel's past suffering.
characters.name: Count Stonuk

characters.description: The pixie who emerges from a long-sheltered chrysalis when Richter plants a blessed seed core. A wordless memory-song reveals her as the daughter of the dead pixie queen, and thus the new Pixie Queen herself. She pledges herself to Richter as a companion and accepts his newly grown celestial tree as her home, a bond she has chosen but not yet performed.
characters.name: Elora

characters.description: A gray-skinned, elf-eared sorceress and necromancer who wields a black staff and raises the dead. She leads part of the war-band that assaults the Mist Village and shares a bitter, unexplained history with Hisako and Yoshi, both of whom recognize her with open hatred.
characters.name: Modara

characters.description: A mountain dwarf blacksmith among the Mist Village's crafters. Gruff, proud, and fond of haggling, he proves to hold deep knowledge of dwarven metallurgy and lore. He pledges fealty to Richter and becomes the village's official smith.
characters.name: Krom

characters.description: A demon that emerges from a sealed sarcophagus deep in the Time Worn Dungeon. Succubus-like but repellent even to other demons, she dominates and commands the dead rather than seducing the living, and revives and empowers the undead guards Richter's party faces.
characters.name: The Crypt Mistress

characters.description: A regal warrior woman of a race called the Tefonim, drawn into the dungeon through a portal Richter opens. She reveals that the undead guards the party fought were once her own honorable soldiers, corrupted over ages; she restores them and her long-lost mate to life and gifts Richter the rare skill of portal construction.
characters.name: Tefonim Queen

characters.description: Sion's childhood best friend, seen only in a forced memory-vision Sion relives inside a dungeon trial. As boys the two crossed into human territory, where Kurian was captured and killed by poachers, an event that long shaped Sion's hostility toward humans.
characters.name: Kurian

characters.description: The unnamed ruler of the Light Court, an alien and highly ritualized elven court that watches the wider world for magical anomalies. Long ago he ordered the plague that exterminated the pixie race, a choice he has begun privately to regret.
characters.name: King of Light
